content: We can model every autonomous agent as a zero-shot node embedded within a unified graph of all possible agent-environment interaction schemas, where the agent can generalize its action capabilities to new unobserved environments without fine-tuning by leveraging relational context from the unified graph. This re-frames agent generalization not as an problem of internal policy iteration, but as a node-level out-of-distribution generalization task over a unified relational structure.
source_topics: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
trigger_topic: Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
